feed.title: AEGISES
feed.subtitle: Capability-secured, typed, default-deny architectures — the substrate is the regulation.

entry.title: PanSigna — Typed Policy Reactor Monitor-Compiler
entry.summary: One reference monitor for a whole machine. Authority is a typed, default-deny policy compiled — not interpreted — through a multi-level IR into CHERI capabilities, seL4 kernel objects, WASM imports, or inline guards, with per-artifact translation validation. Rests on Cedar's Lean-mechanized semantics, seL4 functional correctness, CHERI hardware capability safety, and the MLIR lowering discipline.

entry.title: A Public Policy Ontology for CHERI — From Users to Registers
entry.summary: CHERI enforces policy at the metal and across the stack. The opportunity: one public ontology of authority whose lowering emits into the ecosystem's four existing enforcers — cheriot-audit's Rego, Cedar, eBPF, XDP2 — so a policy written for a user surface reaches the register that carries it, losslessly, and only once.

entry.title: The Custom-Code Gap Map — CHERI-seL4-NixOS
entry.summary: Eight seams no dependency fills on the path from macOS to NixOS in a window on CHERI-seL4. Two greenfield gaps (CHERI × H-extension guest capability state; first execution of QEMU's CHERI+H path), two fork-merges, one delta-completion, three pattern-ports — including the first NixOS ever to boot on seL4.

entry.title: Sovereign Compute Architecture
entry.summary: A capability-typed sovereign compute stack: four machines, one role each. Thirteen stoppable phases from substrate lockdown to SmartNIC/DPU line-rate policy. Own the substrate, gate the platform, outlive the vendors.
